lang.switcher.label: Language
shell.menu.title: Menu
home.page_description: Resource health, scheduled collection, and runtime status across your managed Rebecca and PasarGuard servers.
home.metric.peak_download_title: Peak download (RX) bandwidth. Upload is excluded so this lines up with the download-only port speed VPS providers advertise.
home.empty.snapshots_desc: Open a server's monitoring page and refresh it to start collecting resource snapshots.
home.empty.nodes_desc: The scheduler lists Rebecca and PasarGuard nodes here after its first discovery sweep of registered servers.
home.scheduler.credentials_note: Credentials resolved from external_ref pointers — never stored in the database.
common.name: Name
pagination.label: Pagination
servers.title: Servers
servers.list_description: Your managed Rebecca and PasarGuard servers. Register a target to start collecting monitoring and node data.
servers.toolbar_nodes_title: Node instances discovered across all servers
servers.bulk.confirm: Run this bulk action on the selected servers?
servers.card.delete_confirm: Delete server "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
servers.empty.no_match_desc: Nothing matched "{query}". Try another name, host, or tag.
servers.empty.none_desc: Register your first SSH target to start managing servers.
servers.form.cred_stored: Stored — save password in Nodexia (enables scheduler)
servers.form.cred_external: External reference — env var or file path
servers.form.stored_hint: Stored encrypted at rest. The scheduler uses this to run monitoring automatically.
servers.form.extref_hint: Non-secret pointers resolved at connection time.
servers.form.agent_note: Nodexia will use the SSH_AUTH_SOCK environment variable set at startup. No credentials to enter here.
servers.form.runtime_hint: Leave blank to enter credentials on every request. If filled in, the scheduler can also use this password.
servers.form.quick_access: Quick access to this server's management pages.
servers.form.forget_host_key_confirm: Remove the stored host key? The next connection will trust whatever key the server presents.
servers.form.info_stored_desc: The SSH password is saved in Nodexia. The background scheduler uses it automatically for monitoring (every 15 min) and node discovery (every 12 h).
servers.form.info_runtime_desc: You enter the password each time you open a monitoring or nodes page. If you also fill in the password field, the scheduler can use it too.
servers.form.info_external_desc: Store a non-secret pointer such as password_env=MY_VAR or key_file=/run/secrets/ssh_key. Best for key-based auth and production deployments.
servers.form.info_agent_desc: Nodexia inherits the SSH_AUTH_SOCK socket from its own process environment. No credentials stored.
servers.form.new_description: Add a new SSH target with labels and a non-secret credential handling plan.
servers.form.new_invalid_description: Fix the validation issues below and submit the server again.
servers.form.edit_description: Update the SSH target metadata, labels, and credential handling plan.
servers.form.edit_invalid_description: Fix the validation issues below and save the server again.
servers.form.fix_fields: Please fix the highlighted fields before saving.
servers.flash.host_key_forgotten: Stored host key removed. The next connection will trust and pin the new key.
servers.flash.bulk_no_selection: Select at least one server before running a bulk action.
servers.flash.bulk_invalid_action: Choose a valid bulk action (reboot, update, or delete).
servers.flash.bulk_job_expired: That bulk action result is no longer available (it expired or the server restarted).
servers.error.not_found_message: The requested server record does not exist anymore.
servers.error.invalid_form_message: The submitted server form could not be parsed.
servers.error.create_message: The server record could not be created.
servers.error.load_message_requested: The requested server record could not be loaded.
servers.error.load_message: The server record could not be loaded.
servers.error.update_message: The server record could not be updated.
servers.error.delete_message: The server record could not be deleted.
servers.error.forget_message: The stored host key for this server could not be removed.
servers.error.db_unavailable_message: The servers page cannot load because the database runtime is not available.
servers.error.load_servers_message: The server registry could not be loaded from the persistence layer.
shell.meta_description: Self-hosted control panel for monitoring and managing Rebecca and PasarGuard panel nodes.
shell.footer_note: Open-source, self-hosted monitoring and node management for Rebecca and PasarGuard.
module.current_state_desc: This route group is registered through the new modular application core and is ready for focused implementation in the next phases.
login.title: Login - {app}
login.heading: Administrator login
login.subtitle: Enter your credentials to access the control panel.
login.too_many: Too many failed attempts. Try again in {duration}.
login.tagline: Monitor traffic, watch resources and manage your Rebecca & PasarGuard nodes from one secure place.
diagnostics.title: Diagnostics
diagnostics.page_description: Runtime health, background jobs, and support signals without reading application logs manually.
diagnostics.health_hint: Use the ready endpoint for database-backed probes. Container healthchecks can keep using /healthz.
backup.export.title: Export backup
backup.export.description: Download a portable JSON backup of the panel's configuration: servers, alert rules, channels, and silences. Collected telemetry (metrics, traffic, history) is not included — the collectors rebuild it.
backup.export.warning: By default the backup is secret-free: stored SSH credentials are redacted and no environment values are included. It restores onto either SQLite or MySQL.
backup.export.include_secrets_hint: Adds stored SSH credentials and sensitive environment values (session secret, admin login, Telegram bot token). This produces a sensitive file — handle it securely and prefer encrypting it with a passphrase.
backup.export.passphrase_placeholder: Leave empty for an unencrypted file
backup.export.passphrase_hint: If set, the backup is encrypted with AES-256-GCM and you will need this passphrase to restore. Strongly recommended whenever secrets are included.
backup.restore.title: Restore backup
backup.restore.description: Upload a backup file to restore the panel's configuration on this host.
backup.restore.warning: Restore replaces the current configuration (servers, alert rules, channels, silences). Telemetry tied to removed servers is also cleared. Make an export first. If the backup was made on another host, stored SSH credentials still work because they are not tied to an encryption key — but anything excluded from the backup must be re-entered.
backup.restore.passphrase_hint: Required only if the backup file was encrypted at export time.
backup.restore.confirm: I understand this overwrites the current configuration
backup.restore.confirm_prompt: This will replace the panel's current configuration. Continue?
backup.flash.unavailable: Backup and restore need a configured database.
backup.flash.restore_ok: Backup restored: {servers} servers, {channels} channels, {rules} rules, {silences} silences.
backup.flash.restore_env_note: The backup also carries {count} environment secret(s); these are not applied automatically — set them in this host's environment or .env.
backup.flash.restore_confirm_required: Tick the confirmation box before restoring.
backup.flash.restore_no_file: Choose a backup file to restore.
backup.flash.restore_too_large: That file is too large to be a valid backup.
backup.flash.restore_bad_format: This file is not a recognised Nodexia backup.
backup.flash.restore_unsupported_version: This backup was created by a newer version of Nodexia and cannot be restored.
backup.flash.restore_passphrase_required: This backup is encrypted. Enter the decryption passphrase.
backup.flash.restore_wrong_passphrase: Incorrect passphrase, or the backup file is corrupt.
backup.flash.restore_invalid: The backup is invalid or references servers/channels that are not in the file.
backup.flash.restore_failed: Restore failed and no changes were applied: {error}
bulk.title: Bulk action results
bulk.progress_note: Running in the background — each server updates live as it finishes.
bulk.description_done: Per-server outcome for the bulk {action} operation.
bulk.description_running: The bulk {action} operation is running — this page refreshes automatically.
terminal.title: Terminal
terminal.connecting_stored: Connecting to {addr} — stored credentials will be used automatically.
terminal.connecting_enter: Connecting to {addr} — enter your SSH credentials below.
terminal.init_hint: This terminal will run {cmd} once connected.
files.title: Files
files.page_description: Browse remote directories over SFTP and download files with runtime credentials only.
files.creds_hint: Not persisted — used only for this session
files.browse_hint: Browse a directory to see its contents here.
system.title: System
system.page_description: Collect and review core host metadata such as OS version, kernel, architecture, uptime, and recent package update signals.
system.empty_desc: Collect OS details, kernel version, architecture, uptime, and package update signals over SSH.
system.stored_note: Stored credentials will be used automatically.
commands.title: Commands
commands.page_description: Run one-off SSH commands, stream long output, and review recent command history without persisting secrets.
commands.interactive_note: Interactive command detected — it will open in the Terminal with a real PTY.
commands.no_history: No command history for this server yet.
monitoring.title: Monitoring
monitoring.page_description: Collect CPU, memory, disk, load average, uptime, and network summary snapshots over SSH.
monitoring.silence_title: Mute alert notifications for this server
monitoring.live_title: Live — sampled every {interval}s over a shared SSH session
monitoring.snapshot_title: Showing the latest stored snapshot. Store SSH credentials for this server to stream live.
monitoring.empty_snapshot_desc: Use the refresh panel below to collect CPU, memory, disk, load, and network metrics over SSH.
monitoring.avg_title: Average download (RX) bandwidth. Upload is excluded so this lines up with the download-only port speed VPS providers advertise.
monitoring.vnstat_missing_hint: vnStat may not be installed or the interface has no data yet.
analytics.page_description: Historical metrics, trends, and bandwidth forecasting.
analytics.global_description: Global resource usage and bandwidth consumption across all servers.
analytics.no_data_range: No data yet for this range
analytics.no_traffic: No vnStat traffic data for this server
analytics.no_traffic_sub: Collect a monitoring snapshot to populate traffic history
analytics.forecast_subtitle: Projected download consumption based on historical download-traffic patterns.
analytics.global_empty_desc: Collect at least one monitoring snapshot to populate the analytics overview.
analytics.top_traffic: Top Servers by This Month's Traffic
analytics.limit.title: Monthly Download Limit
analytics.limit.hint: Optional monthly download (RX) cap for this server. When set, the forecast flags exhaustion risk and projects how many days until you reach it.
analytics.limit.error_invalid: Could not read the submitted form.
analytics.limit.error_positive: Enter a download limit greater than zero, or leave it empty to remove the limit.
analytics.limit.error_save: Could not save the limit. Please try again.
analytics.limits.title: Traffic Limits
analytics.limits.description: Set a fleet-wide default monthly download cap and per-tag caps. A server's own per-server limit always takes precedence; otherwise the smallest matching tag cap applies, then the global default.
analytics.limits.banner_desc: Monthly caps feed the forecast and predictive alerts. A server's own limit wins, then the smallest matching tag cap, then the global default.
analytics.limits.precedence_title: How a server's effective cap is picked
analytics.limits.precedence_server_desc: Set on the server's analytics page — always wins.
analytics.limits.precedence_tag_desc: Smallest cap among the server's tags.
analytics.limits.precedence_global_desc: Used when nothing more specific matches.
analytics.limits.global_hint: Applies to every server that has no per-server limit and no matching tag cap.
analytics.limits.tag_hint: Cap every server carrying a tag. When a server matches several tag caps, the smallest one wins.
alerts.overview_description: Define metric thresholds, route notifications to Telegram channels, and mute servers that are intentionally noisy.
alerts.streak_title: Pending breach streak — alert fires when streak reaches {count}
alerts.delete_rule_confirm: Delete this alert rule? This cannot be undone.
alerts.empty_rules_desc: Create a rule to get notified when CPU, RAM, disk, load, or traffic crosses a threshold.
alerts.channels_hint: The Telegram bot token is read from NODEXIA_TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and is never stored here — only the chat id lives in the database.
alerts.send_test_title: Send a test message to this channel
alerts.delete_channel_confirm: Delete this channel? Rules pointing at it will fall back to all enabled channels.
alerts.empty_channels_desc: Add a Telegram channel so fired alerts have somewhere to go.
alerts.no_servers_hint: Register a server first to mute its metrics.
alerts.no_silences: No active silences. Every server reports on every metric.
alerts.no_events: No alerts have fired yet. Rules are evaluated after each background monitoring run.
alerts.rule_form_new_desc: Fire a notification when a metric crosses a threshold. Leave the server unset to apply the rule to every server.
alerts.rule_form_edit_desc: Update the threshold, channel, or noise controls for this rule.
alerts.rule_form_fix_create: Fix the highlighted fields and create the rule again.
alerts.rule_form_fix_save: Fix the highlighted fields and save the rule again.
alerts.fix_fields: Please fix the highlighted fields before saving.
alerts.fix_fields_mute: Please fix the highlighted fields before muting.
alerts.section_scope_hint: Which servers does this rule watch?
alerts.section_condition_hint: Pick a metric and decide when it should alert.
alerts.section_behavior_hint: How fast the rule fires and how often it repeats.
alerts.section_delivery_hint: How the alert is labelled and where it goes.
alerts.condition_days_note: Fires when the forecast projects this many days (or fewer) left before the monthly limit is reached.
alerts.condition_boolean_note: This metric is an event — the rule fires when the condition is detected and resolves when it clears. There is no threshold to configure.
alerts.needs_limit_note: Forecast-based: needs a monthly traffic limit on the server (its own, or an inherited tag/global cap) plus a few days of history. Until then the rule is simply skipped — it never fires.
alerts.needs_history_note: Needs a few days of traffic history to establish a baseline. New servers are skipped until enough data exists.
alerts.consecutive_hits_hint: Consecutive breaching cycles required before the first alert fires. 1 = alert on first breach (recommended). Higher values suppress noise but add delay — with a 15-min monitoring interval, 3 hits means a 45-min wait.
alerts.cooldown_hint: Minimum gap between repeat notifications while still firing.
alerts.note_placeholder: Optional context shown next to the rule
alerts.rule_enabled_hint: Disabled rules keep their configuration but are never evaluated.
alerts.preview_first_breach: fires on the first breaching check
alerts.channel_form_new_desc: Route alert notifications to a Telegram chat or channel.
alerts.channel_form_edit_desc: Update where this channel delivers notifications.
alerts.channel_form_fix_create: Fix the highlighted fields and create the channel again.
alerts.channel_form_fix_save: Fix the highlighted fields and save the channel again.
alerts.chat_id_hint: The chat or channel id alerts are delivered to. The bot token comes from NODEXIA_TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and is never stored.
alerts.message_template_placeholder: Leave blank to use the default alert message.
alerts.test.no_token: Telegram bot token not configured. Set NODEXIA_TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN to send test messages.
alerts.test.render_failed: Could not render the test message — check this channel's template: {error}
alerts.metric_hint.server_unreachable: Fires when a monitoring sweep cannot reach the server over SSH, and resolves as soon as it is reachable again.
alerts.metric_hint.node_stopped: Fires when node discovery finds a PasarGuard or Rebecca node in the stopped state on this server.
alerts.metric_hint.cpu: CPU utilisation from the latest monitoring sweep, as a percentage.
alerts.metric_hint.ram: Memory in use as a percentage of total RAM.
alerts.metric_hint.disk: Root filesystem usage as a percentage of capacity.
alerts.metric_hint.load1: 1-minute load average. Sustained values above the CPU core count usually mean saturation.
alerts.metric_hint.load5: 5-minute load average — smoother than the 1-minute value, good for filtering short spikes.
alerts.metric_hint.load15: 15-minute load average — best for catching sustained overload.
alerts.metric_hint.traffic_total: Traffic transferred this month (download + upload), in GiB.
alerts.metric_hint.bandwidth_mbps: Current download throughput in Mbps from the latest traffic snapshot.
alerts.metric_hint.projected_exceed_limi: Forecast-based: fires once month-end usage is projected to exceed the server's monthly traffic limit.
alerts.metric_hint.days_to_exhaustion: Forecast-based: watches how many days are left until the monthly limit is reached.
alerts.metric_hint.traffic_spike: Fires when today's predicted traffic is at least double the recent daily average.
alerts.metric_hint.unusual_growth: Fires when the projected period total exceeds the previous 30 days by more than 50%.
alerts.error.not_found_message: The requested alert record does not exist anymore.
alerts.error.load_message: The alerts page could not be loaded.
alerts.error.invalid_rule_message: The submitted alert rule form could not be parsed.
alerts.error.create_rule_message: The alert rule could not be created.
alerts.error.rule_not_found_message: The requested alert rule does not exist.
alerts.error.load_rule_message: The requested alert rule could not be loaded.
alerts.error.update_rule_message: The alert rule could not be updated.
alerts.error.delete_rule_message: The alert rule could not be deleted.
alerts.error.invalid_channel_message: The submitted channel form could not be parsed.
alerts.error.create_channel_message: The notification channel could not be created.
alerts.error.channel_not_found_message: The requested channel does not exist.
alerts.error.load_channel_message: The requested channel could not be loaded.
alerts.error.update_channel_message: The notification channel could not be updated.
alerts.error.delete_channel_message: The notification channel could not be deleted.
alerts.error.invalid_silence_message: The submitted silence form could not be parsed.
alerts.error.create_silence_message: The silence could not be created.
alerts.error.silence_not_found_message: The requested silence does not exist.
alerts.error.remove_silence_message: The silence could not be removed.
alerts.error.server_not_found_message: The requested server does not exist.
alerts.error.load_server_message: The selected server could not be loaded.
alerts.error.invalid_silence_metric: The metric to silence is missing or unsupported.
alerts.error.load_servers_message: The server registry could not be loaded.
alerts.error.load_channels_message: The notification channels could not be loaded.
alerts.flash.silenced: Metric silenced for the selected server.
nodes.page_description: Discover PasarGuard and Rebecca node installations over SSH, manage them through their official CLIs, and install new PasarGuard nodes.
nodes.install_page_description: The official PasarGuard install script is running over SSH. After it finishes, the API key and SSL certificate needed to register the node in the panel are shown here.
nodes.action_confirm: Really {action} {name}? This runs the official CLI with auto-confirm and cannot be undone.
nodes.store_creds_manage: Store SSH credentials for this server to manage this node.
nodes.store_creds_view: Store SSH credentials to view this node's API key and certificate.
nodes.empty_none_desc: Discovery ran but no PasarGuard or Rebecca installation was found. Install a PasarGuard node, or inspect the raw probe output below for clues.
nodes.empty_nosnap_desc: Run a discovery sweep to find PasarGuard and Rebecca node installations and manage them from here.
nodes.install_lead: Provision a new node on {server}. PasarGuard installs through the official script; Rebecca installs the dev (beta) channel as a native binary node.
nodes.rebecca_stable_soon_tooltip: Stable Rebecca install is coming soon
nodes.rebecca_install_lead: Installs the dev (beta) Rebecca node as a native binary (systemd) service. Unlike PasarGuard, Rebecca does not generate credentials for you — paste the node install bundle issued by your Rebecca panel below, choose the ports, and the installer wires it up.
nodes.bundle_placeholder:
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
…
-----END CERTIFICATE-----
-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
…
-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
nodes.bundle_hint: Paste the full node install bundle from your Rebecca panel — the certificate followed by its private key. It is sent to the installer over SSH and never stored by Nodexia.
nodes.rebecca_install_confirm: Install the dev (beta) Rebecca node on this server using the bundle you pasted? This runs the official dev binary install script and may take several minutes.
nodes.rebecca_install_note: Runs the official rebecca-node.sh --binary --dev installer, supplies your bundle and ports, and starts the systemd node service. The bundle (including the private key) is request-scoped and never written to the database or logs. Re-run discovery afterwards to manage the node.
nodes.err_ports_equal: The API port must differ from the service port.
nodes.err_bundle_required: Paste the node install bundle from your Rebecca panel.
nodes.err_bundle_pem: The bundle must contain a certificate (-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----) followed by its private key.
nodes.err_channel_disabled: That Rebecca install channel is not available yet.
nodes.err_rebecca_exists: A Rebecca node is already installed on this server.
nodes.err_node_name: Use letters, digits, dot, dash, or underscore (max 64 characters).
nodes.err_node_name_taken: A node with this name already exists on this server — pick another name.
nodes.no_probe_output: No probe output was captured for this run.
nodes.node_name_hint: Each instance gets its own /opt/<name> and /var/lib/<name>, so several nodes can run side by side (node, node2, …).
nodes.api_key_placeholder: Leave blank to auto-generate a UUID
nodes.api_key_hint: Must be a valid UUID. Leave blank and the installer generates one for you.
nodes.install_confirm: Run the official PasarGuard install script on this server? This installs Docker-based services and may take several minutes.
nodes.install_note: Runs the official installer, applies your ports and connection type to /opt/<name>/.env, restarts the node through its CLI, and then shows the API key and SSL certificate needed to register it in the PasarGuard panel.
nodes.install_needs_creds: Installing a node needs stored SSH credentials. Edit the server and store credentials first.
nodes.scope_provider: Each node family has a provider that knows how to discover instances from configuration files and drive the official CLI — node names are never hardcoded and any number of instances is supported.
nodes.scope_pasarguard: PasarGuard nodes are Docker based: every instance owns /opt/<name> (compose + .env) and /var/lib/<name> (certs, xray core). Actions run through pg-node --name <name>; new nodes are installed with the official install script.
nodes.scope_rebecca: Rebecca nodes live under /opt/<name> (default rebecca-node); the version comes from .binary-release.json and ports from .env. Actions run through the rebecca-node CLI. Existing installs are detected and managed, and the dev (beta) channel can be installed from the panel as a native binary node.
nodes.scope_ports: Service port is what the panel uses to reach the node API; API port is the Xray management API; Xray proxy ports carry tunnelled traffic.
nodes.scope_actions: Long actions (update, uninstall, install) run as background sessions with live output — runtime credentials stay request-scoped and the API key / certificate shown after an install are never stored.
nodes.install_streaming: The official install script is running — output streams live below.
nodes.register_title: Register this node in the PasarGuard panel
nodes.register_note: Copy the API key and SSL certificate below into the panel's node registration form. These values are shown once and are not stored by Nodexia.
nodes.cert_missing: The certificate could not be read from /var/lib/{name}/certs/ssl_cert.pem — fetch it manually or run "Renew cert" on the node card.
module.placeholder.alerts: The database runtime is not available yet, so alert rules, channels, and silences cannot load.
module.placeholder.analytics: The analytics module requires the database runtime.
module.placeholder.servers: The database runtime is not available yet, so the server registry cannot load persisted records.
module.placeholder.bulk: Bulk server actions need the database and SSH runtime before they can run.
module.placeholder.commands: The command runner needs the database, SSH runtime, and live stream store before it can execute remote actions.
module.placeholder.files: The file browser needs both the database and SSH runtime to be available before SFTP-backed browsing can start.
module.placeholder.system: The system facts page needs both the database and SSH runtime to collect and store host metadata.
module.placeholder.monitoring: The monitoring page needs both the database and SSH runtime to collect and store resource snapshots.
module.placeholder.nodes: This module requires a database runtime, SSH service, and the live stream store to discover and manage nodes.
module.placeholder.terminal: The interactive terminal requires the database, SSH runtime, and ticket store.
js.confirm_irreversible: This action cannot be undone. Continue?
js.copy.label: Copy
js.bulk.confirm_delete: Delete {noun} from the registry? This cannot be undone.
js.bulk.confirm_reboot: Reboot {noun} now? Active connections will drop.
js.bulk.confirm_update: Update packages on {noun}? This can take several minutes.
js.bulk.confirm_node_restart: Restart every PasarGuard and Rebecca node on {noun}? Active connections will drop briefly.
js.bulk.confirm_node_update: Update every PasarGuard and Rebecca node on {noun}? This can take many minutes per server.
js.analytics.forecast_unavailable: Forecast unavailable — no traffic data collected yet
js.analytics.exhaustion_over: Already over your {limit} limit this month — projected month-end {projected}.
js.analytics.exhaustion_today: On track to reach your {limit} limit today.

js.analytics.exhaustion_in_days.one: On track to reach your {limit} limit in {count} day — {date}.
js.analytics.exhaustion_in_days.other: On track to reach your {limit} limit in {count} days — {date}.

js.analytics.exhaustion_safe: On track to stay under your {limit} limit — projected month-end {projected}.

js.analytics.exhaustion_margin.one: {count} day left in the month before it resets.
js.analytics.exhaustion_margin.other: {count} days left in the month before it resets.

js.files.uploads_failed: Some uploads failed — see details above.
js.files.confirm_delete_dir: Delete folder "{name}" and everything inside it? This cannot be undone.
js.terminal.paste_prompt: Paste text to send to the terminal:
servers.form.traffic_reset_day_hint: Day of the month your provider resets the traffic quota (1 = calendar month). Analytics, forecasts, and limit alerts follow this period.
diagnostics.recent_runs_hint: The latest completed monitoring and node-discovery runs, persisted across restarts. Hover a failed result for the error.
home.warn.limit_over: {server} has already exceeded its traffic limit of {limit} for this period.
home.warn.limit_exhaust: {server} is projected to run out of traffic in {days} day(s) (on {date}) — {reset} day(s) before the period resets.
home.warn.spike: {server} is seeing a traffic spike — today's rate is at least twice its daily average.
home.warn.growth: {server} shows unusual traffic growth — this period is projected more than 50% above the last 30 days.
js.tabs.load_failed_server: Server error — the request failed on the backend.
js.tabs.shortcut_hint: Alt+T new · Alt+W close · Alt+Shift+←/→ switch
